headline: Westgate Village North breaks ground — Trader Joe's leads seven-tenant roster at $60M west Toledo development
paragraphs: Nordstrom announced on May 14 that it will open a 21,000-square-foot Nordstrom Rack at Westgate Village North — the first Nordstrom-brand store ever to serve the Toledo market. The store is scheduled to open in summer 2027. Westgate Village North is a 27-acre retail development at the northwest corner of Central Avenue and Secor Road (3311 Secor Road), developed by Abbell Associates in partnership with the Nikki Group on the former Elder-Beerman site. The Toledo-Lucas County Plan Commission approved the $75 million development in February 2026; the project calls for three to four retail and restaurant buildings aimed at 'new-to-market' national brands.

Nordstrom Rack President Gemma Lionello said the company looks forward to 'being a part of the Toledo community,' while Abbell CEO Liz Holland called Nordstrom 'best-in-class' and the anchor needed to 'bring a first-rate selection' to the west Toledo corridor. No other tenants had been named publicly at the time of the initial announcement.

Mayor Kapszukiewicz and Abbell CEO Liz Holland led a groundbreaking ceremony on June 10 at the Westgate Village North site. The city simultaneously announced the full tenant roster: Trader Joe's (Toledo's first location), Dick's House of Sport, Golf Galaxy, Sierra, Nordstrom Rack, Mission BBQ, and Smoothie King — all either new to Toledo or new to the northwest Ohio market. Also attending the ceremony: Toledo Chief Growth Officer Brandon Sehlhorst, District 5 City Council member Sam Melden, and Washington Local Schools Superintendent Kristine Martin. The total project cost is confirmed at $60 million.

Abbell Associates is partnering with The Nikki Group on an approximately $30 million redevelopment of the adjacent former Elder-Beerman site; additional retail tenants for that parcel are expected to be announced in coming months. No public opening timeline has been announced for either project.
why_it_matters: Trader Joe's is Toledo's most-requested national retailer — it has appeared on community wish lists for years and repeatedly cited a market size gap as the barrier. Landing it as the anchor of a new-build development, alongside Dick's House of Sport, Golf Galaxy, and five other new-to-market names, signals that Toledo's demographic and income profile finally clears national retailers' underwriting thresholds. The $60M project on the former Sears site converts a long-vacant anchor pad into a full retail block in one move. For the west Toledo corridor — which serves Washington Local, Sylvania, and Perrysburg shoppers — this is the most consequential retail announcement in a decade. Watch for the Nikki Group's Elder-Beerman parcel tenant announcements, which will determine whether the two-site development becomes a unified retail district.
